calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-21-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following related matters are heard concurrently: PES-21-304412 and PTR-24-307706.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Nader Fannyan, Esq., for and with Paulette Chiu-Yee Tang, in court, Petitioner.
Hussein Saffouri, Esq., Guardian Ad Litem, for Jameson Mark, Beneficiary.

At the outset, the court notes Ms. Robello and Ms. Brown's absences. At the court's query, Mr. Saffouri reports as to his absence at the previous two hearings. Messrs. Saffouri and Fannyan confirm that Mr. Saffouri has not received payment (without court approval) as disclosed in Petitioner's supplement. The issue of the proposed bond of $48,000 is discussed. Both counsel provide argument regarding the bond issue. Mr. Fannyan requests that bond be waived. Mr. Saffouri waives bond requirement on the record.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is taken under submission with the intent to grant. The court shall further consider the bond issue.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Continued To Feb-19-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-15-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nader Fannyan appeared by zoom for Paulette Tang (administrator).
Sheila Robello appeared by zoom for Rebecca Mark (beneficiary).

Mr. Fannyan stated that the special needs trust matter has a hearing date of November 5, 2024. The court raised the issue of taking the current petition off calendar as there are a lot of procedural issues that have not been addressed. Mrs. Robello asked that the petition not be taken off calendar, stated that she has appeared several times, that the procedural questions do not relate to the special needs trust, that they entered into a settlement agreement a year and a half ago and that she would like the procedural issues addressed or sanctions issued for failure to respond to the probate examiner's notes. The court stated that there will be no sanctions, gave Mr. Fannyan a warning, referred to Probate Code 12205 and informed Mr. Fannyan that there may be a reduction in attorney's fees due to delay. Mr. Fannyan stated that Mrs. Robello can file a petition and that her client can be out of the case. Mrs. Robello asked that Mr. Fannyan file a petition for preliminary distribution, stated that the delays were caused by the administrator and questions why her client should bear the costs of filing a petition when this is something that is ordinarily done by the administrator. The First and Final Report of Personal Representative on Waiver of Account, etc. is off calendar. Status on filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of a report on status of administration is set for February 4, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Santos. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-13-2024 Continued To Oct-15-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nader Fannyan appeared by zoom for Paulette Tang (administrator).
Sheila Robello appeared by zoom for and with Rebekah Mark (beneficiary) by zoom.

The court stated that there are some issues with the petition that need to be corrected, and that it also understands that there was some effort in getting a petition to establish a special needs trust on file, but it didn't happen. Mr. Fannyan stated that the beneficiary receives SSI benefits and that they did not want to jeopardize his ability to continue receiving public benefits. The court informed Mr. Fannyan that a special needs trust is needed. Mr. Fannyan asked if he could proceed with distribution to Mrs. Robello's client. Mrs. Robello stated that the petition was filed on December 7, 2023, that she has no objections to the petition for final distribution, asked the court to consider bifurcating the issue of distribution to her client and to Mr. Fannyan's client, and retain Jameson's interest until the special needs trust is established. The court denied the request for partial distribution without prejudice stating that it can be renewed upon a proper request. The First and Final Report of Personal Representative on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to October 15, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court hopes to have the special needs trust filed by then.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Aguerre. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-02-2024 Continued To Aug-13-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nader Fannyan appeared by zoom for Paulette Tang (administrator.
Sheila Robello appeared by zoom for Rebekah Mark (beneficiary).

The court noted that a number of items have been pointed out by the probate examiner. Mr. Fannyan stated everything is initially done and ready to file, but there is a need for special needs trust for one of the beneficiaries who is on SSI and disabled. Mrs. Robello raised the issue of continued delay, expressed concern about the impact on her client, stated she does not want a long continuance and raised the issue of doing a preliminary distribution. Mr. Fannyan was informed that he needs to file a petition to establish a special needs trust. The First and Final Report of Personal Representative on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to August 13, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Settlement
Off Calendar Oct-26-2022 Off Calendar

Petition To Obtain Possession And Title To Real And Personal Property Under Probate Code Section 850
Off Calendar Oct-26-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nader Fannyan, via Zoom, for Paulette C.Y. Tang, Personal Representative; Vincent Yu, via Zoom, for Rebekah Mark, Objector.

The court confirms with Mr. Fannyan that he recently discussed this matter with John Atallah, attorney for interested party Marriott Vacations, and advises that the interested party has no position regarding the subject matters at this time. Mr. Fannyan reports on two (2) issues that are causing the delay in finalizing the settlement of this matter and advises that dismissals can be filed as soon as those issues are resolved. Mr. Fannyan proposes submitting a stipulation and order to approve settlement. The court advises that it is unable to confirm settlement via a stipulation and order or ex parte application and that the parties must file a new petition to approve settlement agreement to receive a new hearing date from the clerk's office and subsequently file an ex parte application to advance the hearing if needed. The court confirms with counsel that the subject matters can be taken off calendar.

The subject matters are taken off calendar. A Status of Filing of Petition to Approve Settlement is set for Nov-21-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Maloney. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-20-2022 Continued To Oct-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Obtain Possession And Title To Real And Personal Property Under Probate Code Section 850
Off Calendar Jul-20-2022 Continued To Oct-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nader Fannyan, via Zoom, for Paulette Chiu-Yee Tang, Personal Representative; Sheila Robello, via Zoom, for Rebekah Mark, Objector.

Mr. Fannyan confirms the settlement of this matter and of the intent to file a dismissal. Mr. Fannyan advises of the intent to notify the parties holding funds regarding the settlement and asks for a continuance to do so. The court confirms with Mr. Fannyan that he does not plan on filing a Petition to Approve Settlement. Ms. Robello agrees with Mr. Fannyan's report.

The matters are continued to Oct-26-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court instructs counsel to notify the assigned Examiner if the settlement is finalized prior to the next scheduled hearing.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Of Estate.
Off Calendar Jul-05-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nader Fannyan, via Zoom, for Paulette C.Y. Tang, Administrator; Sheila Robello, via Zoom, for Rebekah Mark, Objector; John Atallah, via Zoom, for Marriot Vacations, Interested Party.

Mr. Fannyan advises that a report has been filed with a hearing date of Nov-15-2022. Mr. Fannyan reports that the related 850 petition in this matter has resolved, and the settlement agreement is with the objector for signature. Ms. Robello confirms the preceding. The court and Mr. Fannyan discuss the issue of the Final Inventory and Appraisal ("I&A") stating that only a portion of the estate is included which does not make it a final I&A and that there is no characterization of the real property. Mr. Fannyan advises that he intends to file an updated I&A to show all of the estate assets and characterization of real property. Mr. Fannyan and the court discuss confirming the settlement agreement. The court advises that it is up to the parties to meet and confer regarding confirming the settlement agreement and file a petition to approve the settlement with the court if needed.

After additional discussion, the status on close of estate is taken off calendar. A Status of Final Inventory and Appraisal is set for Nov-15-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Prado. Not Reported.
